login.title: Sign in to forklift
login.description: Manage repository policies, approvals, and scoped tokens.
login.accessNote: Access is limited by your assigned role.
settings.title: Settings
settings.description: Preferences are saved in this browser until account-level settings are available.
settings.cardDescription: These settings apply only to the current browser.
settings.appearanceDescription: Choose a fixed theme or follow your operating system setting.
settings.languageDescription: Store the preferred interface language for this browser.
settings.language.enDescription: Use English as the preferred UI language.
settings.language.koDescription: Use Korean as the preferred UI language.
Name: Name
Copy this token now. It will not be show: Copy this token now. It will not be shown again.
Issue a scoped access token with an expi: Issue a scoped access token with an expiration date and repository permissions.
Tokens expire after at most one year.
Add repository patterns and allowed toke: Add repository patterns and allowed token actions.
Complete all required fields before crea: Complete all required fields before creating a token.
Clients using this token will immediatel: Clients using this token will immediately lose access. This cannot be undone.
Restrict access by client source IP: Restrict access by client source IP
Require approval before serving a packag: Require approval before serving a package
Block versions not yet scanned (block ac: Block versions not yet scanned (block action only)
Gate packages by their declared license: Gate packages by their declared license
Deny licenses (SPDX id, one per line): Deny licenses (SPDX id, one per line)
Allow licenses (SPDX id, one per line; e: Allow licenses (SPDX id, one per line; empty = allow all but denied)
Block versions not yet resolved (block a: Block versions not yet resolved (block action only)
No users have this role. Assign it from: No users have this role. Assign it from a user's detail page.
Read-only: only administrators can creat: Read-only: only administrators can create or revoke tokens.
No roles yet. Create one to grant reposi: No roles yet. Create one to grant repository access.
Define a reusable access profile and opt: Define a reusable access profile and optional repository permissions.
Add repository patterns and allowed acti: Add repository patterns and allowed actions. Wildcards are accepted.
No receivers yet. Add one to start sendi: No receivers yet. Add one to start sending approval alarms.
The webhook URL is never shown again aft: The webhook URL is never shown again after it is saved. Leave it blank when editing to keep the current URL.
The webhook URL cannot be viewed again a: The webhook URL cannot be viewed again after you save it. You can only replace it with a new one. Keep a copy somewhere safe before you save.
Review quarantined package requests acro: Review quarantined package requests across proxy repositories.
Not scanned yet. The scan runs asynchron: Not scanned yet. The scan runs asynchronously after the request is queued; reload in a moment.
No users currently have approve permissi: No users currently have approve permission for this repository.
Letters, digits, dash and underscore onl: Letters, digits, dash and underscore only.
account can sign in to Forklift and use: account can sign in to Forklift and use its credentials to pull and publish artifacts, while
immediately revokes that access by stopp: immediately revokes that access by stopping all sessions and tokens. The account, its tokens and its role assignments are preserved, so disabling is a reversible alternative to deletion that you can undo at any time by re-enabling.
This is the default administrator accoun: This is the default administrator account, created when Forklift first started. Its status is locked active so the system always keeps at least one administrator who can sign in and recover access. It cannot be disabled by anyone, including other administrators.
You are signed in as this account, so yo: You are signed in as this account, so you cannot disable it yourself. This guards against accidentally locking yourself out of Forklift. If this account needs to be disabled, ask another administrator to do it.
Locked after too many failed password at: Locked after too many failed password attempts — unlock it in Account lockout.
Local and OIDC accounts. Open a user to: Local and OIDC accounts. Open a user to map roles, reset the password, or disable access. OIDC users appear automatically at first login.
Create a local account and optionally as: Create a local account and optionally assign an initial role.
OIDC users are created automatically at: OIDC users are created automatically at first login.
A user with no role cannot access reposi: A user with no role cannot access repositories until one is assigned.
Set an initial password for this local a: Set an initial password for this local account.
This role was configured by a Forklift a: This role was configured by a Forklift administrator in the declarative RBAC policy so it cannot be edited here. To change its permissions or delete it ask an administrator to update the policy file and restart forklift.
Users and group mappings holding this ro: Users and group mappings holding this role lose its permissions immediately. This cannot be undone.
Review storage topology, leader election: Review storage topology, leader election state, and fencing token for the active cluster.
Manual failover gracefully steps this le: Manual failover gracefully steps this leader down so a standby is elected and takes over. The leader releases its lease and traffic moves to the new leader. This pod stays Ready and can be re-elected later. Use it for planned maintenance or to drain this pod. Expect a brief in-flight disruption while traffic moves.
A receiver is a webhook (Slack/Mattermos: A receiver is a webhook (Slack/Mattermost-compatible) alerted when a package enters a repository's approval queue. Each repository chooses which receivers to notify in its Settings.
unknown (metadata request blocked before: unknown (metadata request blocked before a version was resolved)
· users who can approve this repository: · users who can approve this repository
Quarantine queue for proxied packages. A: Quarantine queue for proxied packages. Approve or reject pending requests before a proxy serves them, and block specific poisoned versions outright.
Blocks one exact version even when the p: Blocks one exact version even when the package is approved. Applies immediately, including already-cached copies.
A proxy request flows through each gate: A proxy request flows through each gate in this order — the source-IP ACL first, then the supply-chain policies. Dimmed gates are disabled. A
and audit gates only log and let it cont: and audit gates only log and let it continue.
Read-only view. You can review the confi: Read-only view. You can review the configuration but not change it.
Purging removes all cached/hosted artifa: Purging removes all cached/hosted artifacts but keeps the repository and its settings. This cannot be undone.
Deleting a repository removes its cached: Deleting a repository removes its cached/hosted artifacts. This cannot be undone.
· alarm a receiver when a package is qua: · alarm a receiver when a package is quarantined
Selected receivers get a webhook alarm w: Selected receivers get a webhook alarm when a package here enters the approval queue. Manage receivers on the Notifications page.
Licenses are resolved per version from d: Licenses are resolved per version from deps.dev. A denied license, or any license outside a non-empty allow list, triggers the selected action. Matching is case-insensitive.
No role grants access to this repository: No role grants access to this repository. Add a permission on a role (Roles page) whose pattern matches this repository.
No access token reaches this repository.
No cached artifacts yet. Pull through th: No cached artifacts yet. Pull through the endpoint above to populate the cache.
Host and proxy artifacts across Maven, n: Host and proxy artifacts across Maven, npm, Cargo, Go, and PyPI. Configure per-repository caching and supply-chain policies (age cooldown, package approval).
Register a hosted, proxy, or group repos: Register a hosted, proxy, or group repository for package delivery.
Choose the protocol and serving mode. Th: Choose the protocol and serving mode. The generated endpoint follows this selection.
Configure the remote registry and option: Configure the remote registry and optional supply-chain cooldown.
Block versions newer than a cooldown win: Block versions newer than a cooldown window
Use this when newly published packages s: Use this when newly published packages should cool down before serving.
Select member repositories in lookup ord: Select member repositories in lookup order. The first hit wins.
Scoped credentials for package clients,: Scoped credentials for package clients, limited to chosen repositories and actions within your own permissions. Use a token as the password in your package manager (npm
